---
title: "Wooldridge EACSPD -- Ch. 16: Corner solution outcomes and censored regression models (via StatEcon)"
Authors:
- Jeffrey M. Wooldridge
format:
  html:
    embed-resources: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 5
    toc-expand: true
    toc-title: Contents
    toc-location: left
    code-fold: true
    code-tools: true
    code-summary: "Show code"
execute:
  enabled: true
jupyter: julia-1.12
---

Stata examples reproduced from [UCLA OARC — EACSPD Chapter 16](https://stats.oarc.ucla.edu/stata/examples/eacspd/econometric-analysis-of-cross-section-and-panel-data-by-jeffrey-m-wooldridgechapter-16-corner-solution-outcomes-and-censored-regression-models/).

Textbook: Wooldridge, J. M., *Econometric Analysis of Cross Section and Panel Data*, MIT Press.

# Julia setup

```{julia}
using StatEcon
using DataFrames, ReadStatTables, CSV
using Statistics, StatsBase, LinearAlgebra, Printf, Random
using FixedEffectModels, StatsModels, GLM
using FixedEffectModels: Vcov
using CairoMakie
CairoMakie.activate!(type = "png")
using Optim, ForwardDiff
import Distributions
using Distributions: cdf, pdf, quantile, Normal, TDist, FDist, Chisq

# Artifacts this notebook produces go to `output/`.
const OUT = "output"
isdir(OUT) || mkpath(OUT)
outpath(f) = joinpath(OUT, f)
```

# Data

```{julia}
mroz = DataFrames.DataFrame(dataset("mroz"))
size(mroz)
```

## Example 16.3 (mroz.dta)

Annual hours worked is a corner-solution outcome: 325 of the 753 married women
report zero hours. The example (p. 527) contrasts OLS with the Tobit MLE,
constructs a Tobit R-squared from the conditional-mean prediction
`E[y | x] = Φ(xβ/σ)·xβ + σ·φ(xβ/σ)`, and finally compares two nested Tobit
specifications with `fitstat`.

```{julia}
#=
use mroz, clear

reg hours nwifeinc educ exper expersq age kidslt6 kidsge6

      Source |       SS       df       MS              Number of obs =     753
-------------+------------------------------           F(  7,   745) =   38.50
       Model |   151647606     7  21663943.7           Prob > F      =  0.0000
    Residual |   419262118   745  562767.943           R-squared     =  0.2656
-------------+------------------------------           Adj R-squared =  0.2587
       Total |   570909724   752   759188.463           Root MSE      =  750.18

------------------------------------------------------------------------------
       hours |      Coef.   Std. Err.      t    P>|t|     [95% Conf. Interval]
-------------+----------------------------------------------------------------
    nwifeinc |  -3.446636      2.544    -1.35   0.176
        educ |   28.76112   12.95459     2.22   0.027
       exper |   65.67251   9.962983     6.59   0.000
     expersq |  -.7004939   .3245501    -2.16   0.031
         age |  -30.51163   4.363868    -6.99   0.000
     kidslt6 |  -442.0899    58.8466    -7.51   0.000
     kidsge6 |  -32.77923   23.17622    -1.41   0.158
       _cons |   1330.482   270.7846     4.91   0.000
------------------------------------------------------------------------------

tobit hours nwifeinc educ exper expersq age kidslt6 kidsge6, ll(0)

Tobit estimates                                   Number of obs   =        753
                                                  LR chi2(7)      =     271.59
                                                  Prob > chi2     =     0.0000
Log likelihood = -3819.0946                       Pseudo R2       =     0.0343

------------------------------------------------------------------------------
       hours |      Coef.   Std. Err.      t    P>|t|     [95% Conf. Interval]
-------------+----------------------------------------------------------------
    nwifeinc |  -8.814243   4.459096    -1.98   0.048
        educ |   80.64561   21.58322     3.74   0.000
       exper |   131.5643   17.27938     7.61   0.000
     expersq |  -1.864158   .5376615    -3.47   0.001
         age |  -54.40501   7.418496    -7.33   0.000
     kidslt6 |  -894.0217   111.8779    -7.99   0.000
     kidsge6 |    -16.218   38.64136    -0.42   0.675
       _cons |   965.3053   446.4358     2.16   0.031
-------------+----------------------------------------------------------------
         _se |   1122.022   41.57903                   (Ancillary parameter)
------------------------------------------------------------------------------

  Obs. summary:        325  left-censored observations at hours<=0
                       428     uncensored observations

* get the R-squared for tobit model
matrix b = e(b)
local se = el(b,1, 9)
di `se'
1122.0217

predict xb, xb
gen yhat = norm(xb/`se')*xb + `se'*normden(xb/`se')
reg hours yhat

      Source |       SS       df       MS              Number of obs =     753
-------------+------------------------------           F(  1,   751) =  283.78
                                                       Prob > F      =  0.0000
                                                       R-squared     =  0.2742
                                                       Root MSE      =  742.78

------------------------------------------------------------------------------
       hours |      Coef.   Std. Err.      t    P>|t|     [95% Conf. Interval]
-------------+----------------------------------------------------------------
        yhat |   .9634449   .0571918    16.85   0.000
       _cons |   45.52784   49.34596     0.92   0.356
------------------------------------------------------------------------------

* comparing two tobit models
tobit hours nwifeinc educ exper expersq age kidslt6 kidsge6, ll(0)
fitstat, saving(m0)

Log-Lik Intercept Only:      -3954.892     Log-Lik Full Model:      -3819.095
D(744):                       7638.189     LR(7):                     271.594
                                           Prob > LR:                   0.000
McFadden's R2:                   0.034     McFadden's Adj R2:           0.032
Maximum Likelihood R2:           0.303     Cragg & Uhler's R2:          0.303
McKelvey and Zavoina's R2:       0.357
Variance of y*:            1956786.696     Variance of error:     1258932.624
AIC:                            10.168     AIC*n:                    7656.189
BIC:                          2709.885     BIC':                     -225.226

tobit hours nwifeinc educ exper expersq age kidslt6 kidsge6  unem city, ll(0)

Tobit estimates                                   Number of obs   =        753
                                                  LR chi2(9)      =     274.01
                                                  Prob > chi2     =     0.0000
Log likelihood = -3817.8867                       Pseudo R2       =     0.0346

------------------------------------------------------------------------------
       hours |      Coef.   Std. Err.
-------------+----------------------------------------------------------------
    nwifeinc |  -8.700003   4.544473
        educ |   83.11418   21.68775
       exper |   133.4305   17.31206
     expersq |  -1.934666   .5393865
         age |  -53.13497   7.468394
     kidslt6 |  -887.5036   111.6144
     kidsge6 |  -13.28932   38.60969
        unem |   -23.3407   15.06687
        city |   11.36026   99.61766
       _cons |   1060.127   449.8033
-------------+----------------------------------------------------------------
         _se |   1119.474                                (Ancillary parameter)
------------------------------------------------------------------------------

fitstat, using(m0)

                                Current        Saved       Difference
Log-Lik Intercept Only:         -3954.892     -3954.892        0.000
Log-Lik Full Model:             -3817.887     -3819.095        1.208
D:                            7635.773(742) 7638.189(744)    2.416(2)
LR:                            274.010(9)    271.594(7)      2.416(2)
Prob > LR:                       0.000         0.000          0.299
McFadden's R2:                   0.035         0.034          0.000
McFadden's Adj R2:               0.032         0.032         -0.000
Maximum Likelihood R2:           0.305         0.303          0.002
Cragg & Uhler's R2:              0.305         0.303          0.002
McKelvey and Zavoina's R2:       0.359         0.357          0.003
AIC:                            10.170        10.168          0.002
BIC:                          2720.717      2709.885         10.833

Difference of 10.833 in BIC' provides very strong support for saved model.
=#
```

### OLS on hours

```{julia}
XH = [:nwifeinc, :educ, :exper, :expersq, :age, :kidslt6, :kidsge6]
hours_ols = stata_regress(mroz; y = :hours, x = XH)
```

**Comparison.** Every coefficient and standard error reproduces the published
output exactly (`nwifeinc` −3.446636 / 2.544 … `_cons` 1330.482 / 270.7846),
together with `Number of obs = 753`, `F(7,745) = 38.50`, `R-squared = 0.2656`
and `Root MSE = 750.18`.

### Tobit, `ll(0)`

```{julia}
tb = stata_tobit(mroz, :hours, XH; ll = 0.0, vce = :oim)
```

```{julia}
Printf.@printf("_se (sigma)      = %.7g   (Std. Err. %.7g)\n", tb.sigma, tb.se_σ)
Printf.@printf("Log likelihood   = %.4f\n", tb.ll)
Printf.@printf("left-censored    = %d\nuncensored       = %d\n",
               sum(Float64.(mroz.hours) .<= 0), sum(Float64.(mroz.hours) .> 0))
```

The header statistics Stata prints (`LR chi2(7)` and pseudo R²) need the
intercept-only Tobit, which we fit here as well:

```{julia}
tb_null = stata_tobit(mroz, :hours, Symbol[]; ll = 0.0, quiet = true)
LR7 = 2 * (tb.ll - tb_null.ll)
Printf.@printf("Log-Lik Intercept Only = %.4f\n", tb_null.ll)
Printf.@printf("LR chi2(7)             = %.2f\n", LR7)
Printf.@printf("Prob > chi2            = %.4f\n",
               1 - Distributions.cdf(Distributions.Chisq(7), LR7))
Printf.@printf("Pseudo R2              = %.4f\n", 1 - tb.ll / tb_null.ll)
```

**Comparison.** Coefficients match Stata to seven significant digits
(`nwifeinc` −8.814243, `educ` 80.64561, `exper` 131.5643, `expersq` −1.864158,
`age` −54.40501, `kidslt6` −894.0217, `kidsge6` −16.218, `_cons` 965.3053),
σ̂ = 1122.022 with SE 41.57905 (Stata 41.57903), log-likelihood −3819.0946,
`LR chi2(7) = 271.59`, pseudo R² 0.0343, and the 325 / 428 censoring split.
The standard errors differ only in the sixth significant digit (e.g. `nwifeinc`
4.459053 vs Stata's 4.459096) because `stata_tobit` forms the observed
information from a finite-difference Hessian rather than Stata's analytic one.

### Tobit R-squared

```{julia}
Φ(z) = Distributions.cdf(Distributions.Normal(), z)
φ(z) = Distributions.pdf(Distributions.Normal(), z)

xb  = tb.X * tb.β
σ̂   = tb.sigma
Printf.@printf("di `se' = %.4f\n", σ̂)

tobit_fit = DataFrames.DataFrame(
    hours = Float64.(mroz.hours),
    yhat  = Φ.(xb ./ σ̂) .* xb .+ σ̂ .* φ.(xb ./ σ̂))

r2_reg = stata_regress(tobit_fit; y = :hours, x = [:yhat])
```

**Comparison.** `di \`se'` gives 1122.0217 as in Stata. The auxiliary
regression reproduces `R-squared = 0.2742`, `F(1,751) = 283.78`,
`Root MSE = 742.78`, `yhat` = .9634450 (Stata .9634449) with SE .0571918, and
`_cons` = 45.52784 with SE 49.34596. So the Tobit conditional mean explains
27.4% of the variation in hours, against the OLS 26.6%.

### Comparing the two Tobit models

```{julia}
tb2 = stata_tobit(mroz, :hours, vcat(XH, [:unem, :city]); ll = 0.0, vce = :oim)
Printf.@printf("\n_se (sigma) = %.7g    Log likelihood = %.4f\n", tb2.sigma, tb2.ll)
```

```{julia}
lrt = stata_lrtest(tb2.ll, tb.ll, 2)
```

`fitstat` reimplemented for the two nested Tobits (Long & Freese's definitions):

```{julia}
function tobit_fitstat(res, ll0, N)
    kpar = length(res.β) + 1                   # β (incl. _cons) plus σ
    kx   = length(res.β) - 1                   # slopes only
    D    = -2 * res.ll
    LR   = 2 * (res.ll - ll0)
    xbv  = res.X * res.β
    varxb = Statistics.var(xbv)                # Stata's fitstat uses the N-1 variance
    vare  = res.sigma^2
    (; ll0, ll = res.ll, D, dfD = N - kpar, LR, dfLR = kx,
       pLR   = 1 - Distributions.cdf(Distributions.Chisq(kx), LR),
       mcf   = 1 - res.ll / ll0,
       mcfa  = 1 - (res.ll - kpar) / ll0,
       mlr2  = 1 - exp(2 * (ll0 - res.ll) / N),
       cu    = (1 - exp(2 * (ll0 - res.ll) / N)) / (1 - exp(2 * ll0 / N)),
       mz    = varxb / (varxb + vare),
       varys = varxb + vare, vare,
       aic   = (D + 2 * kpar) / N, aicn = D + 2 * kpar,
       bic   = D - (N - kpar) * log(N), bicp = -LR + kx * log(N))
end

N   = tb.n
fs0 = tobit_fitstat(tb,  tb_null.ll, N)
fs1 = tobit_fitstat(tb2, tb_null.ll, N)

Printf.@printf("%-30s%13s%13s%13s\n", "", "Current", "Saved", "Difference")
Printf.@printf("%-30s%13.3f%13.3f%13.3f\n", "Log-Lik Intercept Only:", fs1.ll0, fs0.ll0, fs1.ll0 - fs0.ll0)
Printf.@printf("%-30s%13.3f%13.3f%13.3f\n", "Log-Lik Full Model:", fs1.ll, fs0.ll, fs1.ll - fs0.ll)
Printf.@printf("%-30s%9.3f(%d)%9.3f(%d)%9.3f(%d)\n", "D:", fs1.D, fs1.dfD, fs0.D, fs0.dfD,
               fs0.D - fs1.D, fs0.dfD - fs1.dfD)
Printf.@printf("%-30s%9.3f(%d)%11.3f(%d)%11.3f(%d)\n", "LR:", fs1.LR, fs1.dfLR, fs0.LR, fs0.dfLR,
               fs1.LR - fs0.LR, fs1.dfLR - fs0.dfLR)
Printf.@printf("%-30s%13.3f%13.3f%13.3f\n", "Prob > LR:", fs1.pLR, fs0.pLR, lrt.p)
Printf.@printf("%-30s%13.3f%13.3f%13.3f\n", "McFadden's R2:", fs1.mcf, fs0.mcf, fs1.mcf - fs0.mcf)
Printf.@printf("%-30s%13.3f%13.3f%13.3f\n", "McFadden's Adj R2:", fs1.mcfa, fs0.mcfa, fs1.mcfa - fs0.mcfa)
Printf.@printf("%-30s%13.3f%13.3f%13.3f\n", "Maximum Likelihood R2:", fs1.mlr2, fs0.mlr2, fs1.mlr2 - fs0.mlr2)
Printf.@printf("%-30s%13.3f%13.3f%13.3f\n", "Cragg & Uhler's R2:", fs1.cu, fs0.cu, fs1.cu - fs0.cu)
Printf.@printf("%-30s%13.3f%13.3f%13.3f\n", "McKelvey and Zavoina's R2:", fs1.mz, fs0.mz, fs1.mz - fs0.mz)
Printf.@printf("%-30s%13.3f%13.3f%13.3f\n", "Variance of y*:", fs1.varys, fs0.varys, fs1.varys - fs0.varys)
Printf.@printf("%-30s%13.3f%13.3f%13.3f\n", "Variance of error:", fs1.vare, fs0.vare, fs1.vare - fs0.vare)
Printf.@printf("%-30s%13.3f%13.3f%13.3f\n", "AIC:", fs1.aic, fs0.aic, fs1.aic - fs0.aic)
Printf.@printf("%-30s%13.3f%13.3f%13.3f\n", "BIC:", fs1.bic, fs0.bic, fs1.bic - fs0.bic)
Printf.@printf("%-30s%13.3f%13.3f%13.3f\n", "BIC':", fs1.bicp, fs0.bicp, fs1.bicp - fs0.bicp)
```

**Comparison.** The larger Tobit reproduces Stata's coefficients to six or seven
significant digits (`unem` −23.34072 vs −23.3407, `city` 11.36005 vs 11.36026,
`_cons` 1060.113 vs 1060.127), σ̂ = 1119.474 and log-likelihood −3817.8867 —
all matching. Standard errors again differ only in the last printed digits,
except `city`, where the FD-Hessian OIM gives 100.6341 against Stata's 99.61766
(≈1% apart); `city` is the flattest direction of the likelihood
(t ≈ 0.11), so it is the coefficient most sensitive to the numerical Hessian.

The likelihood-ratio comparison matches the published `fitstat` block:
log-likelihood difference 1.208, `LR difference = 2.416` on 2 d.f.,
`Prob > LR: 0.299`, and a BIC difference of **10.833** in favour of the saved
(smaller) model. `unem` and `city` do not belong in the equation. The
reconstructed `fitstat` panel reproduces every published line, including
`Variance of y*` (1956786.689 here vs 1956786.696 in Stata — a last-digit
consequence of σ̂ agreeing only to ~9 digits), `Variance of error`
(1258932.622 vs 1258932.624),
`McKelvey and Zavoina's R2 = 0.357/0.359`, `AIC = 10.168/10.170`,
`BIC = 2709.885/2720.717` and `BIC' = -225.226`.
